text.title: Python Ecosystem Overview — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every python-overview application must obey.

rule.statement: Always classify the domain (web / data / ML / automation / embedded) before loading any framework-specific methodology.
rule.rationale: Loading FastAPI methodology for a data-cleaning script wastes context and produces irrelevant rules.

rule.statement: Lock one Python version per domain (web → 3.13, data → 3.13, ML → matches torch matrix). Document the lock in the decision record.
rule.rationale: Different domains have different ecosystem version pressures; locking per domain keeps the toolchain coherent.

rule.statement: Default package manager is uv unless the team has explicit reason otherwise.
rule.rationale: uv is 10–100x faster than pip/poetry, has a single lockfile, and supports workspaces.

rule.statement: Load only methodologies that match the chosen domain. Do not preload Django methodologies for a FastAPI project.
rule.rationale: Mixed methodologies introduce conflicting rules and waste context.

rule.statement: One project, one primary framework. Multi-framework routing must be split into separate sub-projects with their own decision records.
rule.rationale: Cohabiting frameworks in one repo doubles config burden and creates conflicting conventions.
